one_liner: Gorgias is a customer support/helpdesk platform offering a REST API (Basic auth via API key or OAuth2 for public apps) that is self-serve for existing customers and has an official MCP server.

auth_methods.notes: Private apps use HTTP Basic Auth with email as username and API key as password, base64-encoded in Authorization header.
Mandatory for public apps; uses Bearer tokens after OAuth2 authorization flow.

self_serve_notes: Existing Gorgias customers (account owner or admins) can generate API keys directly from helpdesk Settings > Account > REST API, available on all Helpdesk plans, no free tier but 7-day trial without credit card. For third-party developers building public/OAuth2 apps, must register on Developer Portal, create a draft app, and contact techpartners@gorgias.com to convert a trial into a Developer Sandbox account, adding a partial admin-approval step for that path.

api_surface.notes: Full REST API for helpdesk tickets, customers, and account settings; API key/basic auth usable directly from API reference; Postman collection available.

evidence.supports: Describes two auth methods: private app API key (Basic auth) and public app OAuth2 (Bearer token).
Details how to access/create REST API credentials (Basic auth with email + API key) from helpdesk settings, available on all Helpdesk plans.
Describes developer portal registration and sandbox account process for building public/OAuth2 apps.
OAuth2 setup process for public apps.

flags: Official MCP server exists at mcp.gorgias.com
No free tier; 7-day trial only for new accounts
Public/OAuth2 app development requires manual approval via techpartners@gorgias.com for sandbox account, though private app API key self-serve is instant for existing customers
